title: Services, EndpointSlices, DNS, Discovery, and Traffic Policies · Object and Event Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective service discovery and endpoint evidence map; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in services, endpointslices, dns, discovery, and traffic policies, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in service discovery and endpoint evidence map and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Explain Pod IP identity, node routing, ports, host network exceptions, replacement, dual-stack boundaries, and application bind behavior.
Trace Service selectors, EndpointSlices, readiness and serving conditions, manual endpoints, topology hints, and stale endpoint diagnosis.
Choose ClusterIP, headless, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName, or selectorless Service from discovery, exposure, platform, and security constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy
steps.instruction:
Order the K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy orchestration evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy.
steps.why: Correct order keeps K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy, keep K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A debug Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Evaluate session affinity, internal and external traffic policies, source preservation, locality, health checks, uneven load, and failure-domain tradeoffs.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Order the regression proof · Debug K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Evaluate session affinity, internal and external traffic policies, source preservation, locality, health checks, uneven load, and failure-domain tradeoffs.

For Order the regression proof · Debug K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Local traffic policy always improves availability and balances requests evenly.

options.text: Capture client and server versions, context, namespace, API discovery, current object state, ownership, and immutable release identity for the debug K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy case

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-traffic-session-locality-policy-1wm1hx". Tailor its service-discovery.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy, keep K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-traffic-session-locality-policy-1wm1hx
  - competency: k8s-traffic-session-locality-policy
    artifact: service-discovery.yaml
    decision: traffic session locality policy
    environment: three-node dual-stack staging cluster across two zones
    fault-injection: delete one ready Pod during a configuration rollout
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: 1wm1hx
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Service
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      type: ClusterIP
      selector: {app: status-api}
      endpoint-evidence: ready-serving-terminating
      dns: status-api.intake.svc.cluster.local
    verify: kubectl get service,endpointslice -l kubernetes.io/service-name=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject Pod IP pinning, short-name cross-namespace assumptions, Service-as-health-probe claims, or locality policy as automatic availability.
    repair: Align selectors, inspect EndpointSlice conditions, use qualified DNS, test bind and route layers, and document platform-specific exposure.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
